references.title: Fuzzy-based active learning for predicting student academic performance using autoML: a step-wise approach
references.journal: Journal of Computing in Higher Education

extracted.Courses: 3 courses: Physics III, Phjysical Chemistry I & Analtical Chemistry Labs
extracted.Data sources: LMS logs, Intermediate and Final Grades
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Features are extracted from the logs themselves using a plugin.

These are subsequently given to a set of fuzzy learning algorithms.

At the second stage authors 4 distinc strategies:

Uncertainty Sample with LC, Hinted SVM, QUIRE and Random Sampling.
extracted.Models: FMARR, MFPC AIWA/OWA, FPC GA/LGA, PT, PTTD, FPC OWA, Naive Bayesm IBk-5NN, Random Forest, GBoost
extracted.Performance Metric: Best Model: {Friedman Aligned Rank Accuracy: 11.2 - PT,  Friedman Aligned Rank AUC: 15 - PTTD, Friedman Aligned Rank AUC: 15.1 - PTTD}
extracted.Comments:
Authors use AutoML to do hyper.-parameter tuning od different fuzzy learning algorithms to predict a student’s ability to pass our fail a course.

Even after comparison using Standard ML algorithms, the rank performance of the Fuzzy Classifiers beats the remaining models.

Tests using the different forms of resampling show that Hinted SVM or QUIRE are decent techniques.
extracted.Limitations:
No consideration of adding other data sources.

No explanation of learning using resources not available in the LMS.

Uncreative feature engineering and extraction.

annotations.text: While ML to predict student performance is popular, there is a growing need to create models that are accurate but at a low-cost, something fuzzy-models can do. Then, it is important to choose optimal method and optimal parameters for a model. Authors explore the potential of AutoML to predict student performance. Multiple experiments seem to show approach is accurate.
